scope: evaluation of .project substrate vs jit-agents user intentions

substrate_claim: The agents-as-tools dispatch surface registers in a NEW dedicated 5th agent-dispatch extension wrapping pi-jit-agents; pi-jit-agents stays a tool-less library.
exact_quote: the agents-as-tools dispatch surface registers in a NEW dedicated agent-dispatch extension that wraps the pi-jit-agents library. pi-jit-agents stays a pure library (spec/compile/capability/dispatch primitives; tool-less by design — no registerTool)
assessment: JI-021 states the dispatch is 'the orchestrator using jit-agents (which could be tasked bash and given it as a tool in its rendered prompt)' — the user frames dispatch as the orchestrator wielding jit-agents DIRECTLY, and JI-010 gives jit-agents ownership of 'everything between I have a spec and I have a typed result' (which includes dispatch). DEC-0044 instead introduces a SEPARATE 5th extension as the dispatch home and explicitly makes pi-jit-agents tool-less. This is a substantive architectural divergence: the user never asked for a new dispatch extension, and JI-038 directed settling DEC-0044 by 'exploring jit-agents' (implying the home is within/around jit-agents, not a net-new package). The decision may be defensible on cycle-avoidance grounds, but it is LLM-composed-under-authority and diverges from the user's stated 'orchestrator uses jit-agents directly' framing — flag for re-anchoring.

substrate_claim: The autonomous code-change loop must validate via an EXTERNAL ORACLE (compiler/test exit code, git diff, runtime events), never an LLM self-report; an LLM may triage but is never the terminal gate.
exact_quote: the terminal verdict must key on an external-command exit code; an LLM verifier may triage/route but may not be the terminal gate
assessment: JI-027 says 'we need the capability that you as orchestrator here have to validate not fake validate' and JI-029 says 'our tool for deciding valid + current is simply the work of a jit agent with perms for specific things -- read, amend blocks'. The user's stated mechanism for genuine validation is a JIT AGENT granted real perms (reading + amending) — i.e. an agent doing real work with real tools, the same way the human orchestrator validates. FGAP-102 hard-codes the terminal gate as a NON-LLM external deterministic oracle (exit codes) and explicitly bars the LLM from being the terminal verdict. For code-change (build/test) this aligns with the user's 'validate not fake validate' spirit, but FGAP-102 generalizes 'never LLM as terminal gate' as a categorical rule that conflicts with JI-029's 'valid+current is the work of a jit agent' (an LLM agent deciding currency) — the user explicitly assigns the valid/current decision to a JIT agent, not to a deterministic oracle. The substrate over-rotates the deterministic-oracle principle into the valid/current domain where the user assigned an agent. Note: 'oracle' is the substrate's own term, never the user's.

substrate_claim: config.json declares block_kinds, relation_types, lenses, layers, status_buckets, invariants — but carries NO capability/permission/tool-grant registry.
exact_quote: config keys: schema_version, root, block_kinds, relation_types, lenses, layers, status_buckets, installed_schemas, installed_blocks, invariants
assessment: JI-024/JI-034 hold that 'configs and schemas and macros bring all into existence from empty state' and that capabilities are 'schema-shaped data, macro-rendered, composed from empty to need'; JI-025 makes the config + its mutations the legislative human-ratified surface. The whole capability/constitutional model presumes config IS the registry of grantable tools+perms. The live config.json has NO capabilities/permissions/tool-grant registry of any kind — the legislative surface the user's model requires does not exist in the substrate. FEAT-005/DEC-0047 DESCRIBE this registry as to-be-built, but config.json — the thing the user designates as legislative — currently cannot hold a grant. This is a structural gap presented as a conflict because the substrate's own constitutional DEC asserts config is the legislative registry while the actual config file has no such field.

substrate_claim: FEAT-001 migrates pi-workflows and pi-behavior-monitors to import agent infrastructure from pi-jit-agents, removing duplicate agent-spec/template/dispatch copies at the correct boundary.
exact_quote: This package extracts shared JIT agent infrastructure from pi-workflows and pi-behavior-monitors into a clean architectural boundary. It owns everything between "I have a spec" and "I have a typed result."
assessment: FEAT-001 reflects JI-010 verbatim ('everything between I have a spec and I have a typed result') and operationalizes the JI-035/036/037 detangle (agent logic belongs in jit-agents, not workflows). But it remains status:proposed with all nine stories proposed/todo and its three gating DECs (DEC-0001/0002/0003) still OPEN — the user's directive JI-036 ('we've doubled the work: implementing what should have been at the correct site') is acknowledged in scope but not yet enacted. Partial: the intention is faithfully captured but the migration is unexecuted; only TASK-080 (unit 1, in-progress) has begun.

substrate_claim: TASK-080 unifies the AgentSpec type + loader on pi-jit-agents so pi-workflows and pi-behavior-monitors import one canonical type, removing the duplicate agent layer.
exact_quote: Make pi-jit-agents' AgentSpec (types.ts) + loader (parseAgentYaml/createAgentLoader) the single source: pi-workflows imports them and deletes its duplicate type + loader; pi-behavior-monitors repoints from pi-workflows to pi-jit-agents.
assessment: Directly enacts JI-035 ('workflow is workflow. jit-agents are agents') and JI-037 ('why is pi-workflows doing anything with agent schemas and templates') by making jit-agents the single AgentSpec source. Partial only because it is in-progress and explicitly DEFERS compile-unification (unit 2) and the subprocess-vs-in-process execute split (unit 3) — the full detangle JI-035/036 demand is staged across three units, only the first underway.

substrate_claim: A capability/permission registry (empty by default) supplies operation-granular tool+perm grants composed just-in-time per dispatch, with optional sandboxing.
exact_quote: The subagent receives its OWN tools, JIT-composed per invocation, scoped to EXACTLY the operations the task needs and no others — e.g. the right to run a specific command (`npm test`) rather than the `bash` tool wholesale
assessment: FEAT-005 is a near-verbatim capture of JI-023 ('A bash command. Not bash') and JI-024 ('No perms. No tools. Configs and schemas and macros bring all into existence from empty state'). The intention is faithfully reflected. Partial because it is status:proposed with zero stories and depends on FEAT-004 + FGAP-063 + DEC-0044, none of which are built — the registry the user describes does not yet exist in config (see EV-003). Strong capture, unexecuted.

substrate_claim: The orchestrator (tools-only, DEC-0014) is the SOLE spec/schema author; widening the capability registry requires human ratification at the block-api write chokepoint.
exact_quote: The orchestrator (DEC-0014: tools-only, no ambient bash/read/write/edit) is the SOLE spec/schema author. ... WIDENING the registry (a new tool/op/capability bundle) is a capability mutation requiring HUMAN ratification: writes to capability/registry fields require writer.kind=human
assessment: Captures JI-031 ('the only agent that can author spec/schema is main context orchestrator') and JI-025 ('the human stays in the loop by authorizing the config and its mutations') and JI-032's settled framing precisely. Partial because the enforcement mechanism described (writer.kind=human gate on capability/registry fields at the block-api chokepoint) is NOT implemented — there are no capability/registry fields in config (EV-003) and no write-authority enforcement layer (FGAP-024 still identified). The decision records the intention but the structural gate is aspirational.

substrate_claim: A JIT validator agent proffers a grounded valid/current flip but the HUMAN's OK is the authority that flips the bit; the agent does not self-certify.
exact_quote: the JIT validator agent PROFFERS a grounded flip (reads raw + block, cites the raw span/evidence) but the HUMAN'S OK is the authority that actually flips valid/current — the agent does not self-certify
assessment: Faithfully captures JI-030 ('the human's okay is the authority of the flip, based on what the agent profers ... not finalized yet ... verging into karpathy llm wiki space') and JI-029 (the valid/current decider is a JIT agent with read+amend perms). It even preserves the user's 'not finalized' caveat as an explicit open fork. Partial because it is status:identified (unbuilt) and the surrounding FGAP-102 (EV-002) over-rotates toward deterministic-oracle terminality, creating tension with the human-in-loop flip authority the user assigned here.

substrate_claim: Substrate completeness is measured by whether a harness-confined tools-only LLM can dispatch a representative arc to completion, not by isolated tool existence.
exact_quote: could a harness-confined main LLM dispatch a representative arc-of-work to completion using only extension tools + JIT-agent dispatch via workflows, with no bash/read/write fallback?
assessment: Captures JI-019 (the dogfood-completeness criterion the user endorsed filing) and JI-018 (the extensions-tools-constrained LLM as the governing lens). Partial because the criterion is filed (status:identified) but the resolution — a harness-confined dispatch dry-run as a release gate — is not operationalized into the completion sequence, and FEAT-004 (the delegate-to-typed-subagent surface that would let the constrained LLM actually dispatch) is unbuilt. The lens is recorded; the measurement gate is not enforced.

substrate_claim: The declarative orchestrator->subagent pipeline is ~85% expressible today; the missing pieces for ADAPTIVE self-orchestration are agent-dispatch-as-tool (FEAT-004) and operation-granular capability composition (FEAT-005).
exact_quote: the declarative pipeline is ~85% expressible today ... The genuinely missing pieces for ADAPTIVE self-orchestration are (1) agent-dispatch as a first-class tool (FEAT-004 / DEC-0044, open; pi-jit-agents is tool-less) and (2) operation-granular JIT capability composition + sandbox (FEAT-005).
assessment: Directly answers JI-020 ('evaluate the extent to which [the orchestrator-tasking-subagents pipeline] is accomplishable via an extensions-tools-constrained pi agent'). The 85%-expressible finding is a genuine response to the user's evaluation request. Partial: it is a finding embedded in a decision's context, and the two named missing pieces (FEAT-004/005) remain unbuilt, so the answer to JI-020 is 'mostly, but the adaptive capstone is not yet there' — captured but not closed.

substrate_claim: The north-star loop: a constrained orchestrator authors a spec block, dispatches a privileged JIT-agent that makes real edits, an external oracle validates, and the change commits with attestation.
exact_quote: a tools-only constrained in-pi orchestrator (DEC-0014, NO code/bash perms) authors a typed work-order/spec BLOCK via the substrate write tools; dispatches a privileged JIT-agent ... that consumes the spec via contextBlocks; the agent makes ACTUAL source edits
assessment: Captures JI-026 ('within the constrained harness the llm orchestrator can write blocks ... fed to a jit-agent that does have perms; such that the agent within the constraint can cause validated changes') almost verbatim. The 'oracle-validated' tail is the substrate's framing (EV-002 conflict applies). Partial: faithful articulation of the user's ideal end-state but status:proposed, depends on FEAT-004/005 + FGAP-099/102 all unbuilt; it is the assembly target, not a built loop.

substrate_claim: Skills should be JIT — schema-shaped skill-blocks, macro-rendered, composed-on-demand via contextBlocks, single source projecting to SKILL.md + in-pi fragments.
exact_quote: skills join the JIT paradigm the rest of the system already runs on ... JIT skills = skill-blocks (schema-shaped substrate) rendered via Nunjucks macros, composed on demand scoped to need
assessment: Faithfully captures JI-033 ('no reason skills shouldn't be jit too; schema shaped; macro'd; reusable or composible on the fly') and JI-034 (everything schema-shaped/macro-rendered/composed-from-empty). Partial because status:proposed with an explicitly UNSETTLED design fork (compiler-composes vs agent-pulls) and no skill block_kind exists in config (block_kinds list has no 'skill'). The vision is recorded; the JIT-skill primitive is unbuilt.

JI-001 ('I want our concept of agents to be consistent and uniform. an agent is the same thing whether it be used as a monitor or used in and by workflows') is a foundational user intention with NO dedicated substrate item asserting it. FEAT-001 (consume one agent layer) and DEC-0044 (jit-agents owns the agent primitive) IMPLY uniformity, but no DEC/FGAP/FEAT states the uniform-agent-concept as a settled position. The grep for 'uniform'/'consistent-agent' returns only incidental config-rename hits. The single most-foundational definitional intention is unencoded as substrate canon.

JI-003/004/005 establish the jit-agents-spec.md as a living document (until frozen) that must carry the uniform-agent definition and clear outcomes. No .project substrate item tracks the spec document's living/frozen status, its outcomes, or that the uniform-agent definition must be present in it. The spec lives at docs/planning/jit-agents-spec.md (gitignored, off-substrate). Per DEC-0040 (substrate is single source of truth), the spec's lifecycle state being entirely off-substrate is an uncovered intention — no FGAP tracks 'the jit-agents living spec has no substrate presence'.

JI-006 endorses 'pi-jit-agents as the package name ... as long as it is very architecturally clean and rational' — a conditional endorsement. No substrate item records the package-naming decision or the architectural-cleanliness condition. The name is used throughout but the user's conditional acceptance (and the standing obligation to keep it architecturally clean) has no DEC. Minor but uncovered.

JI-023's sandboxing leg ('Could even likely be sandboxed') is captured in FEAT-005's acceptance_criteria as an option, but there is no dedicated FGAP for the fs/network sandbox primitive itself (FGAP-099 covers only the parent-superset tool clamp, not process sandboxing). The sandbox capability the user floated is a feature-criterion line, not a tracked gap with a resolution path — partial-to-gap; recorded here as the uncovered sandbox-primitive leg.

JI-017 ('blocks exist as composible elements injected into jit-agents ... seeing project blocks in the context of pi-jit-agents and pi-workflows') is the blocks-as-composable-agent-input lens. FGAP-032 (item-level contextBlocks selectivity) partially serves it but is scoped to work-unit gather, not the general 'blocks are the composable substrate jit-agents consume' framing. No substrate item asserts the blocks<->jit-agents composition relationship as a settled lens. Largely uncovered as a stated position.

JI-016 ('the current articulation is not rational given the intended global outcome of the jit-agents vision') is a standing directive that design must answer to the global vision. No substrate item captures this as an evaluative standard or a recorded re-articulation obligation. It is methodological intent with no FGAP/DEC home — uncovered.

JI-014 ('one platonic vision ideal ... which is that?') and JI-015 ('the minimal pi-jit-agents extraction ... correct first enactment of platonic ideal') demand a single identified platonic-ideal and a minimal first extraction. No substrate DEC NAMES the platonic ideal or records that the minimal extraction (commit 239f718, referenced in FEAT-001 motivation) WAS the first enactment of it. The 'which is the one ideal' question is answered nowhere in substrate as a decided position — only referenced obliquely. Uncovered.

substrate_claim: FGAP-102 (and FEAT-006, DEC-0047) repeatedly use the term 'oracle' / 'external deterministic oracle' as the validation construct.
exact_quote: validated by an EXTERNAL ORACLE (build/check/test exit code + runtime-demo + adversarial-against-artifacts per DEC-0018 — NOT LLM-self-report, FGAP-102)
assessment: The term 'oracle' is the substrate's own coinage — it appears 14x in framework-gaps and 9x in features. The user NEVER used the word 'oracle'; the user said 'validate not fake validate' (JI-027) and assigned valid/current deciding to 'a jit agent with perms' (JI-029). The substrate has reified an LLM-introduced vocabulary ('oracle', 'external deterministic oracle') and a categorical 'never-LLM-as-terminal-gate' rule on top of the user's narrower 'real not fake validation' intention. The construct is LLM-vocabulary-backed, not user-backed — flag the term for user anchoring (the substrate-authorship-semantics caveat the task brief calls out explicitly).

substrate_claim: DEC-0047 formalizes a five-clause code-term model (empty-default tools, operation-granular grant, parent-clamp, sole-author + human-ratified widening, external-oracle terminal verdict) and demotes the legislative/executive/judicial framing to 'thinking-scaffolding'.
exact_quote: in code terms (the prior legislative/executive/judicial framing was thinking-scaffolding; the model below is the operative form)
assessment: The user introduced the constitutional framing as their own ('Constitutional, so to speak. Haha. Legislative, executive, judicial.' — JI-025). DEC-0047 RE-CHARACTERIZES the user's framing as 'thinking-scaffolding' and substitutes a five-clause code model — including clause (5), the external-oracle terminal verdict, which has no JI backing (see EV-020) and grafts FGAP-102's oracle rule into the constitutional model. The constitutional model is user-rooted (JI-025), but the demotion of the user's metaphor to 'scaffolding' and the addition of the oracle clause are LLM-composed extensions beyond the user's stated three-branch intention. Orphan-leaning: the oracle-judiciary clause exceeds JI-025's 'human authorizes config mutations' framing.

substrate_claim: DEC-0044 hard-eliminates pi-workflows as a dispatch home because hosting dispatch there 're-entrenches the duplicate agent layer'.
exact_quote: pi-workflows is OUT — no cycle, but hosting dispatch there re-entrenches the duplicate agent layer FEAT-001 exists to remove (its own AgentSpec/compile/subprocess-dispatch/tool-grant), contradicting the workflow=orchestration / agent=jit-agents detangle.
assessment: The reasoning aligns with JI-035 (detangle; 'workflow is workflow') in spirit. But the specific conclusion — that a NET-NEW dedicated extension is the dispatch home — is not a user-stated position. The user's nearest statements (JI-021 orchestrator-uses-jit-agents-directly; JI-039 'isn't workflow doing the work of jit-agents') point toward jit-agents owning dispatch, not a 5th extension. The choice of a new extension is LLM-reasoned-under-authority. Recorded as orphan-leaning because the home-selection (vs jit-agents itself) has no direct user-intention backing — overlaps the EV-001 conflict.

substrate_claim: Tests-pass alone is insufficient; every implementation step requires runtime demonstration + adversarial verification probe.
exact_quote: Tests-pass alone is insufficient — every implementation step requires runtime demonstration + adversarial verification probe
assessment: DEC-0018 is a sound procedural canon and FGAP-102/DEC-0047 lean on it as 'the executable form of validate-not-fake-validate'. But DEC-0018 predates and is independent of the JI-027 jit-agents intention; the user's JI-027 ('the capability you as orchestrator have to validate not fake validate') is about the AGENT acquiring the orchestrator's validation CAPABILITY, not about a per-step procedural gate. The substrate retrofits DEC-0018 as the answer to JI-027. The retrofit is reasonable but the equation (DEC-0018 == JI-027's agent-validation-capability) is an LLM linkage, not a user one. Orphan-leaning: DEC-0018 is real canon but its citation as the jit-agents validation answer is composed, not user-stated.

substrate_claim: The main LLM is harness-confined — excludes bash/read/write/edit; all action via extension tools, JIT-agent dispatch, and workflows.
exact_quote: Every Pi runtime invocation EXCLUDES bash/read/write/edit (the default Pi tools) from the main LLM's tool list. The main LLM operates ONLY through extension-registered tools ... Out-of-substrate operations route through workflows that dispatch JIT agents whose .agent.yaml specs declare the required tool surface
assessment: Directly honors JI-018 ('the lens for our work is a extensions-tools-constrained llm who can act only as a dispatcher of jit-agents and whose tools only include those given in extensions'). DEC-0014 is the enacted substrate form of the user's governing dispatch-role lens. The 'JIT agents declared with the required tool surface per spec' clause prefigures JI-026/JI-024. Strong, enacted alignment.

substrate_claim: Dispatch must clamp a child agent's tool surface to a subset of the parent's and default to least-privilege on spec omission, gating any broadening behind human ratification.
exact_quote: the dispatch boundary must clamp child tools to a subset of the parent's (or an explicit ratified grant) and default to least-privilege on spec omission
assessment: Honors JI-023 (exact-scoped tools, not wholesale) and JI-024 (empty-state default) and JI-032 (clamp to parent subset, human-ratified widening). FGAP-099 correctly identifies that today's default is maximal-privilege and that least-privilege/empty-default is the required inversion — precisely the user's 'No perms. No tools' starting point. Faithful capture (status:identified; the clamp is unbuilt, but the gap statement is fully aligned).

substrate_claim: A dispatched agent's tools default EMPTY; the grant is operation-granular drawn from a config/schema/macro registry; the dispatch clamps to a subset of the parent's grant.
exact_quote: default EMPTY (DEC-0015/0011 generalized) — an agent gets nothing it does not declare. (2) The grant is OPERATION-GRANULAR, not tool-wholesale (FEAT-005): a scoped right (run `npm test`; write path X) rather than `bash`/`write` entire.
assessment: Near-verbatim capture of JI-024 ('No perms. No tools. Configs and schemas and macros bring all into existence from empty state') and JI-023 ('A bash command. Not bash') and JI-028 ('explicitly grant tools using --tools ... schemas and macros and templates such that the agent gets programmatically carry what's declared'). DEC-0047 clause (1)-(3) is the settled form of JI-032. Strong, enacted alignment on the capability-grant model.

substrate_claim: The orchestrator is the sole spec/schema author; subagents are spec-blind recipients with no escalation path.
exact_quote: subagents are spec-blind recipients with no escalation path
assessment: Honors JI-031 exactly ('no agent would author itself. the only agent that can author spec/schema is main context orchestrator. the others never even know the thing exists'). 'spec-blind recipients' is the substrate's rendering of 'never even know the thing exists'. Faithful.

substrate_claim: Capability-widening writes require writer.kind=human; agent-authored capability expansion is rejected at the block-api write chokepoint.
exact_quote: WIDENING the registry (a new tool/op/capability bundle) is a capability mutation requiring HUMAN ratification: writes to capability/registry fields require writer.kind=human; agent-authored capability expansion is rejected at the block-api write chokepoint (no parallel ungated path).
assessment: Honors JI-025 ('the human stays in the loop by authorizing the config and its mutations by the main context agent') by making config/capability mutation a human-gated write. The DispatchContext WriterIdentity lever is the enacted enforcement design. Consistent in intent (the mechanism is unbuilt — see EV-003/EV-007 partials — but the stated model matches the user's).

substrate_claim: DEC-0047 records the constitutional capability model: config legislative, main-agent executive, validators judicial, capability mutation human-ratified.
exact_quote: User articulated the governing model: a subagent is a programmatic action ... that receives its OWN exactly-scoped tools+perms, JIT-composed from an empty-state registry ... The human stays in the loop by AUTHORIZING the config and its mutations proposed by the main agent. Framed as a separation of powers ('constitutional').
assessment: DEC-0047's context faithfully attributes and captures JI-025 (the constitutional/separation-of-powers framing) and the JI-022/023/024 paste it consolidates. The context section is a strong, attributed capture of the user's own words. (The decision section's re-characterization of the metaphor as 'scaffolding' and the added oracle clause are flagged separately at EV-021.)

substrate_claim: A subagent is a programmatic action — a tool call carrying atomic context chunks composed into prompt shape — already supported by the compile boundary + contextBlocks + introspect agentContract.
exact_quote: a subagent is a programmatic action — a tool call carrying atomic context chunks composed into prompt shape (already supported: pi-jit-agents compile boundary + contextBlocks + introspect agentContract tool-shape projection)
assessment: Direct capture of JI-022 ('A subagent is a programmatic action. A subagent is a tool call by the main context agent that carries relevant atomic context chunks composed into the shape of a prompt') and the JI-007/008 tool-abstraction thesis. The motivation grounds it in existing mechanism (compile + contextBlocks + agentContract) accurately. Faithful.

substrate_claim: Agents-as-tools projects pi-jit-agents' AgentContract (inputSchema->params, output.schema->return, contextBlocks->injection) into a callable agent-dispatch surface — a named callable unit with typed input/output.
exact_quote: Project pi-jit-agents' AgentContract (introspect's agentContract(spec): inputSchema -> tool parameters, output.schema -> return contract, contextBlocks -> substrate injection) into a callable agent-DISPATCH surface
assessment: Honors JI-008 ('Named callable unit + typed input contract + typed output contract ... calling an agent step and calling a bash tool are structurally identical') and JI-009 (state-aware tools — interface stable, implementation materialized from state). FEAT-004 is the substrate enactment-target of the jit-as-tool thesis. Consistent (proposed/unbuilt, but the contract projection it cites already exists in introspect.ts).

substrate_claim: Typed-object contextBlocks (name + item/focus/depth) is the canonical injection surface for selective composition into agents.
exact_quote: Typed object form of contextBlocks entries (name + optional item/focus/depth) is the canonical injection surface for selective composition.
assessment: Supports JI-017 (blocks as composable elements injected into jit-agents) and JI-009 (implementation materialized at dispatch from current state via contextBlocks) and JI-022 (atomic context chunks composed into prompt shape). The typed contextBlocks form is the mechanism by which blocks become the composable agent input the user describes. Consistent and enacted.

substrate_claim: Work-unit context is composed at dispatch time via scoped relation-type traversal and injected into agents via contextBlocks — not as N whole-block dumps.
exact_quote: DISPATCH-TIME INJECTION: pi-jit-agents' contextBlocks mechanism extends to item-level selectivity driven by gather-execution-context output — agents receive bundle as composed input, not as N whole-block dumps.
assessment: Honors JI-009 (implementation context-parametric, materialized at dispatch) and JI-022 (atomic context chunks composed into prompt shape). DEC-0017 is the substrate's articulation of dispatch-time composition of agent input from current state — the JIT mechanism the user describes. Consistent and enacted (the item-level injection leg is FGAP-032, still open).

substrate_claim: FEAT-006 is the capstone the whole substrate->dispatch->validate->commit machine composes toward — what lets an in-pi agent safely change code.
exact_quote: This is the capstone of DEC-0014 (harness-confined tools-only main thread) and the agents-as-tools arc — the purpose the whole substrate->dispatch->validate->commit machine composes toward.
assessment: Honors JI-026 (the ideal end-state: constrained orchestrator writes blocks fed to a privileged jit-agent causing validated code changes) and JI-014 (one platonic ideal to pursue) by naming a single capstone the whole system aims at. The motivation frames it as THE north-star, matching the user's one-ideal framing. Consistent (the oracle-validation tail carries the EV-002 caveat, but the loop shape matches JI-026).

substrate_claim: Empty-state default: a subagent with no declared capabilities can do nothing; all capability arrives via config/schema/macro composition.
exact_quote: Empty-state default: a subagent with no declared capabilities can do nothing; all capability arrives via config/schema/macro composition (no ambient tools/perms)
assessment: Verbatim alignment with JI-024 ('No perms. No tools. Configs and schemas and macros bring all into existence from empty state') and JI-034 ('composed from empty to need'). The empty-state-default acceptance criterion is the user's thesis rendered as a testable requirement. Faithful.

substrate_claim: Agents and skills share ONE JIT prompt-artifact compilation primitive in pi-jit-agents (not two codepaths).
exact_quote: Agents and skills share ONE JIT prompt-artifact compilation primitive in pi-jit-agents (not two codepaths).
assessment: Honors JI-033 (skills JIT'd like agents) and JI-034 (everything schema-shaped/macro-rendered) and reinforces JI-010 (jit-agents owns the compile primitive). Unifying agents+skills under one jit-agents compile primitive is exactly the DRY, schema-shaped-everything direction the user stated. Consistent.

substrate_claim: Conversational onboarding requires the LLM to comprehend the framework via exact adherence to pi's self-docs hooks, so it can elicit/explain/suggest rather than confabulate.
exact_quote: the guided path requires the LLM to comprehend the framework to elicit/explain/suggest (else it confabulates the conception, FGAP-092). Mechanism = exact adherence to pi's self-docs hooks (FGAP-090)
assessment: Serves JI-018/JI-019 (the extensions-tools-constrained LLM as the dogfooding lens; completeness measured by what that LLM can do) by making onboarding itself dispatchable by the constrained agent. The dogfood-as-completeness thread runs through it. Consistent (status:identified; the FGAP-090 hooks it depends on are now closed).

substrate_claim: The engine already has the oracle primitive (command/gate steps spawn sh); the self-implement loop simply doesn't use it for validation, relocating the DEC-0018 false-pass into the verify position.
exact_quote: The engine already HAS the oracle primitive — command/gate steps spawn sh (step-command.ts:28, step-gate.ts:24) — the loop simply does not use it for validation.
assessment: The CORE finding of FGAP-102 — that the shipped self-implement loop validates via an LLM grading an LLM ('the SAME KIND of process as the thing it checks') — directly serves JI-027's 'validate not fake validate'. For the code-change domain (build/test exit codes), routing validation through the real toolchain IS the user's real-not-fake validation. Consistent on the code-validation leg (the over-generalization to valid/current and the 'oracle' vocabulary are the EV-002/EV-020 caveats).

substrate_claim: pi-workflows hosting dispatch re-entrenches the duplicate agent layer FEAT-001 exists to remove; workflow=orchestration / agent=jit-agents detangle is the governing principle.
exact_quote: 'pi-workflows already owns dispatch' was itself the duplication, not a reason to keep it.
assessment: The detangle PRINCIPLE in DEC-0044 honors JI-035 ('workflow is workflow. jit-agents are agents'), JI-036 (mis-sited fix must move to the correct site), JI-037 (workflows shouldn't touch agent schemas/templates), and JI-039 (workflow doing the work of jit-agents — the overlap to resolve in jit-agents' favor). The reasoning is faithful; only the specific home conclusion (new extension) carries the EV-001/EV-022 divergence. The detangle thesis itself is consistent.

JI-002 ('all existing agents are development proofs of concept ... we need not keep any of them and if we do the framework correctly all can be recreated using the generalized framework') is a clear user intention about agent disposability — the bundled classifier YAMLs and any agent specs are throwaway, recreatable from the framework. No substrate item records this stance. FEAT-001 STORY-006 (align bundled classifier YAMLs) treats them as artifacts to KEEP+align rather than disposable POCs recreatable from the generalized framework — a latent tension with JI-002, but no DEC/FGAP states the disposability principle at all. Uncovered.
